response: Japan, a leading advanced global economy home to 125 million people, boasts a $4.2 trillion total GDP that expands at a 1.1% annual growth rate, while recording a very low 2.6% unemployment rate and a 3.2% inflation rate. The nation also carries a substantial fiscal burden with a 263% debt-to-GDP ratio, ranks among the highest in the world for its 84.6 year life expectancy, and relies on $756 billion in annual exports to power its world-renowned industrial and technology sectors.
